athlete_profile: Age: 41, Sex: Female, Height: 165 cm, Weight: 58 kg, Running experience: 5 years. Experienced runner with multiple half-marathon finishes (PB 1:38). Runs 4-5 times per week, including trail and road. Has completed one marathon previously (3:45 in 2024). No current injuries but history of mild IT band tightness. VO2max estimated at 52.0 mL/kg/min. Resting heart rate: 48 bpm. Average HRV: 65 ms.
goal: Preparing for the Amsterdam Marathon on October 18, 2026. Target finish time: sub 3:30 (previous marathon: 3:45). Wants to improve endurance and speed work to close the 15-minute gap.
user_message: My long runs feel comfortable but I'm struggling with speed work. My tempo pace has plateaued around 4:30/km for the past few months and I can't seem to push through. I also wonder if I should increase my weekly mileage. What should I focus on to get to sub 3:30?
training_data:
Last 4 weeks summary:
- Week of Feb 23: 3 runs, 34.0 km total, avg pace 4:48/km, avg HR 148 bpm
- Week of Feb 16: 4 runs, 42.0 km total, avg pace 4:52/km, avg HR 150 bpm
- Week of Feb 9: 4 runs, 45.0 km total, avg pace 4:55/km, avg HR 149 bpm
- Week of Feb 2: 3 runs, 36.0 km total, avg pace 4:50/km, avg HR 151 bpm

Trends: Consistent weekly mileage around 35-45 km. Pace is stable in the 4:48-4:55/km range. Heart rate well-controlled on easy runs (140-148 bpm). Long run has been building (up to 16 km). Speed sessions show HR hitting 174-176 bpm, indicating good intensity. Cadence is solid (176-184 spm). Longest recent run: 16.0 km (76.8 min).
additional_context: Runner has a strong aerobic base with good VO2max (52 mL/kg/min) for her age. Sleep quality is excellent (7.8h avg with good deep/REM ratios). HRV of 65 ms indicates good recovery capacity. Previous marathon was 3:45, so a sub-3:30 target requires roughly 4:58/km pace — achievable given current fitness but will need specific marathon pace work. History of IT band issues means hip/glute strengthening should be maintained. Currently averaging 35-45 km/week; will need to build toward 50-60 km/week for marathon prep.
plan_context: Goal type: race. Race distance: marathon (42.195 km). Race date: 2026-10-18. Available training weeks: approximately 33 weeks from today. The athlete is experienced with one previous marathon (3:45). A 16-week specific marathon block is recommended, preceded by a base-building phase. Target race pace: 4:58/km. Plan should include progressive long runs up to 32-35 km, marathon-pace tempo sessions, and VO2max intervals. 4 sessions per week recommended, with a 2-3 week taper.
